heading: National congenital heart disease research, surveillance, and awareness
label: 280g–13 National congenital heart disease research, surveillance, and awareness
notes: Amendments2024—Subsecs. (f), (g). , (2), added subsec. (f) and redesignated former subsec. (f) as (g). Former subsec. (g) redesignated (h).Pub. L. 118–107, § 2(1)Subsec. children.text: enhance and expand research and data collection efforts related to congenital heart disease, including to study and track the epidemiology of congenital heart disease to understand health outcomes for individuals with congenital heart disease across all ages;
conduct activities to improve public awareness of, and education related to, congenital heart disease, including care of individuals with such disease; and
award grants to entities to undertake the activities described in this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the incidence and prevalence of congenital heart disease in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: causation and risk factors associated with, and natural history of, congenital heart disease;

children.label: C
children.text: health care utilization by individuals with congenital heart disease;

children.label: D
children.text: demographic factors associated with congenital heart disease, such as age, race, ethnicity, sex, and family history of individuals who are diagnosed with the disease; and

children.label: E
children.text: evidence-based practices related to care and treatment for individuals with congenital heart disease.

children.label: A
children.text: collect data on the health outcomes, including behavioral and mental health outcomes, of a diverse population of individuals of all ages with congenital heart disease, such that analysis of the outcomes will inform evidence-based practices for individuals with congenital heart disease; and

children.label: B
children.text: consider health disparities among individuals with congenital heart disease, which may include the consideration of prenatal exposures.

children.heading: Awareness campaign
children.label: c Awareness campaign
children.text: The Secretary may carry out awareness and educational activities related to congenital heart disease in individuals of all ages, which may include information for patients, family members, and health care providers, on topics such as the prevalence of such disease, the effect of such disease on individuals of all ages, and the importance of long-term, specialized care for individuals with such disease.

children.heading: Public access
children.label: d Public access
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that, subject to subsection (e), information collected under this section is made available, as appropriate, to the public, including researchers.

children.heading: Patient privacy
children.label: e Patient privacy
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the data and information collected under this section are made available in a manner that, at a minimum, protects personal privacy to the extent required by applicable Federal and State law.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of past and present activities of the Department of Health and Human Services to increase awareness and knowledge of the public with respect to congenital heart disease, including efforts to address the lifelong needs of congenital heart disease patients.

children.label: B
children.text: An assessment of past and present activities of the Department of Health and Human Services to increase education and training of health care providers with respect to congenital heart disease, including efforts to address the lifelong needs of congenital heart disease patients.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the current workforce capacity in the United States of health care providers who treat adult patients living with congenital heart disease.

children.label: i
children.text: address any public awareness and research gaps and opportunities related to the lifelong needs of congenital heart disease patients, including long-term health outcomes, quality of life, mental health, and health care utilization;

children.label: ii
children.text: address any shortages in the current workforce of health care providers who treat adult patients living with congenital heart disease, which may include strategies to enhance fellowship training programs or other continuing education programs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: foster collaboration and dissemination of information across Federal agencies, health care providers, researchers, and patient organizations.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: B Consultation
children.text: In developing the strategy under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall, as appropriate, consult with qualified stakeholder groups, including patient organizations, health care professionals, research entities, health insurance providers, accrediting organizations, and relevant Federal agencies, including the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the National Institutes of Health, and the Health Resources and Services Administration.
be a public or private nonprofit entity with specialized experience in congenital heart disease; and
submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2025 through 2029.
